codeReview.summary: The feature introduces the `--remote` flag and a DataSource abstraction, but core remote-mode behavior does not match the hosted API contract, so multiple MCP tools cannot function correctly in remote mode.

issues.description: `fetch_remote_spikes` only parses either a raw JSON array or an object containing a `spikes` field, but the worker API returns paginated payloads as `{ data: [...], next_cursor: ... }`. This causes remote read tools to return empty/missing results instead of real spikes (see worker contract: `spikes-hosted/worker/src/handlers/spikes.ts` lines 203, 252-254).

Remote `resolve_spike`/`delete_spike` call `PATCH /spikes/:id` and `DELETE /spikes/:id`, but the worker router only defines `GET /spikes/:id` (and no spike PATCH/DELETE routes), so these tools will fail in remote mode (see `spikes-hosted/worker/src/index.ts` lines 76-78).

`submit_spike_remote` only sends `projectKey` when `project_key` is provided. The MCP arg is optional, but the worker schema requires at least one of `project` or `projectKey`; therefore valid MCP calls (page+comments only) can fail validation remotely (see `spikes-hosted/worker/src/schema.ts` lines 58-61 and 81-83).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `worker-cli` skill does not strongly enforce API-contract verification against `../spikes-hosted/worker` for remote-mode features, which allowed incorrect endpoint/response assumptions to ship.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for session `04788f64-02f2-4591-bab5-0499c408ad0e` shows only CLI file reads and DataSource-focused tests; no worker route/schema reads and no remote API contract tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared library docs capture the DataSource concept but not key remote API wire contracts (e.g., GET /spikes returns paginated `data`, and spike mutation endpoints are not exposed), which increases risk of contract drift.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/architecture.md` mentions DataSource pattern but omits response/endpoint specifics; worker implementation shows actual contract in `spikes-hosted/worker/src/handlers/spikes.ts` (lines 203, 252-254) and `spikes-hosted/worker/src/index.ts` (lines 76-78).

summary: Review result: FAIL. Remote mode scaffolding exists, but remote read parsing and remote mutation endpoint assumptions are incompatible with the hosted API contract, and submit_spike can fail schema validation when `project_key` is omitted.
